message:
test(fcp-conformance/quorum-policy): pin Byzantine quorum thresholds + degraded-mode admission (br-n83l4)

QuorumPolicy + required_quorum are the BFT primitives that drive
AuditHead / ZoneCheckpoint signature verification, zone lease grants,
and degraded-mode admission. The thresholds are NORMATIVE — any drift
silently weakens Byzantine resilience. Zero conformance coverage.

Adds 16 tests against the public fcp_core::quorum API:

- 4× per-tier threshold pinning across (n, f) ∈ {(1,0), (3,1), (5,2),
  (10,3), (100,33)}: Safe=1, Risky=f+1, Dangerous=n-f,
  CriticalWrite=n-f
- dangerous_and_critical_write_thresholds_are_equal (the documented
  shared n-f threshold; drift would weaken one tier silently)
- required_quorum_is_monotone_across_tiers (Safe ≤ Risky ≤
  Dangerous; the lattice that makes risk classification meaningful)
- is_quorum_met at, above, below threshold (Dangerous tier)
- is_degraded under/at/above eligible_nodes
- can_proceed_degraded conjunction (allow_degraded_mode=true AND
  available>=min AND tier==Safe — ALL three gates required, any
  one false rejects). 4 specific reject paths tested.
- can_proceed_degraded_rejects_non_safe_risk_tiers (Risky /
  Dangerous / CriticalWrite all rejected — only Safe ops allowed
  in degraded mode)
- quorum_policy_required_signatures_matches_required_quorum_free_fn
  (the policy method MUST delegate to the free function — divergence
  would fork the threshold semantics between policy-bound and
  policy-less paths)
- risk_tier_as_str labels (admin-tooling stable strings)
- single-node zone edge case (n=1, f=0)

All 16 pass locally in <0.01s via cargo test -p fcp-conformance --test
quorum_policy_conformance.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
